response.justification: Wei Chen is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling and IDE-adjacent background (worked on NI LabWindows/CVI, built test runners and developer libraries), but the core requirements of this role are largely unmet: there is no evidence of Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise Java framework experience, and only minimal Java exposure years ago. His depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM ecosystem, making him a weak fit for a role demanding deep JVM, Java, and Spring expertise despite his solid craftsmanship credentials.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling instincts — built test runners, assertion libraries, and MCP code generators
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and long track record across many stacks
concerns: No Kotlin experience and no evidence of deep JVM knowledge
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework background
Java exposure is minimal and dated (side projects at Altom, ~2016-2018)

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling interest
